name: UK Drill
description: Fast-paced UK drill with rapid hi-hats, punchy drums, dark atmosphere. Typical BPM 140-180.

energy_curve.description: Minimal, sparse intro with dark ambience
Peak energy with full drum pattern

variation_moves.name: hat_triplet_roll
variation_moves.description: Rapid triplet hi-hat roll (signature UK drill move)

variation_moves.name: kick_syncopation
variation_moves.description: Syncopated kick pattern with unexpected hits

variation_moves.name: snare_chop
variation_moves.description: Snare sample chopped up and looped

variation_moves.name: drum_fill
variation_moves.description: Traditional drum fill before section change

verse_to_hook.description: Upward filter sweep with bass drop

hook_to_verse.description: Snappy drum fill into the next section

kick.notes: Two-layer kick with sub + punch click

bass.notes: Deep, dark bass with movement, minimal mid-range

snare.notes: Punchy, cutting snare sound. Can add reverb for interest

hi_hat.notes: Fast, crisp closed hats. Very prominent in the mix

hat_roll.notes: Trademark UK drill rapid hat rolls

production_notes.key_characteristic: Rapid hi-hat work with minimal rest, dark atmosphere
typical_elements: Hook samples or vocal chops for identity
production_notes.arrangement_strategy: Build energy through hat patterns and bass, not drum quantity. Use silence strategically. Maintain hypnotic loop feel.
production_notes.energy_sustain: High throughout verse sections (0.6-0.85), needs variation in hat patterns rather than adding new instruments
production_notes.signature_move: Triplet hi-hat roll that defines UK drill sound

breakdown.intro_8: Sparse drums, dark pad, establishing vibe
breakdown.verse_16: Full groove with rapid hats, hook sample intro
breakdown.hook_8: Peak energy section with acapella or hook
breakdown.verse_16: Variation on first verse, slower hats section
Final big verse with maximum energy variations
